Top-left text, two lines in large black serif font:

- “Cogsci/Psychology 127: Lecture 9”
- “Object Recognition”

Background plain light beige/off-white.

Near the top center-right, a small screen-recording control overlay with tiny icons and labels, including a blue circular button and a red X close button. Visible labels/icons in the overlay are too small to read clearly except for partial interface elements.

Top left text, two lines in large serif font:

“Cogsci/Psychology 127: Lecture”
“Object Recognition”

Mostly blank slide otherwise.

Small black cursor/arrow near the center, slightly above midpoint.

Top right floating annotation toolbar with small icons and labels:
blue circular icon at left, then icons labeled “Mouse”, “Draw”, “Erase”, “Camera”, “Shapes”, “Text”, and a red “X” close icon.

Bottom left tiny video/player overlay icons in a row:
speaker/volume icon, magnifying glass icons, and several small circular control icons.

Title at top: “Cortical visual areas form two pathways”

Large labels:
- “akinetopsia” above center
- “Dorsal” at right upper-middle
- “Ventral” at right lower-middle
- “achromatopsia” below center

Left diagram: lateral view of macaque brain labeled “Macaque”
Cortical region labels on brain:
- “Parietal cortex” at upper rear
- “Occipital cortex” at rear
- “Inferotemporal cortex” at lower side
Colored arrows/paths on brain:
- blue path projecting upward/backward toward parietal cortex
- pink path projecting forward/downward toward inferotemporal cortex

Main pathway schematic below/right of brain:
- leftmost yellow box: “Retina”
- connected to yellow box: “LGN”
- then to small pale yellow box: “V1”
- from V1 downward to small pale yellow box: “V2”
- from V1/V2 region to pale yellow box: “V3”
Background divided into regions:
- green upper/right region for dorsal pathway
- pink lower/right region for ventral pathway
- gray left region around early visual areas

Dorsal/green pathway nodes:
- white oval/box: “PO” near upper center-left of green region
- white irregular box: “MT” left-center in green region, circled with thick yellow outline
- white oval: “MST” center-right in green region
- white box: “VIP” upper-right in green region
- white box: “7a” far right in green region
- white oval: “LIP” lower-right in green region
- curved arrows connecting MT, MST, VIP, 7a, LIP, and PO

Ventral/pink pathway nodes:
- large pale yellow oval: “V4” near lower center, circled with thick blue outline
- white box: “TEO” to right of V4
- white box: “TE” lower-right
- label “Inferotemporal cortex” printed vertically/diagonally near TEO/TE region
- arrows from V4 toward TEO and TE

Other labels:
- “Occipital cortex” printed near left side of the green/pink schematic, above V1/V2/V3 area

Colored emphasis marks:
- thick yellow oval around MT with yellow pointer line upward to the word “akinetopsia”
- thick blue oval around V4 with blue pointer line downward to the word “achromatopsia”

Small toolbar overlay at very top center-right with tiny icons and labels:
- “Mouse”
- “Draw”
- “Erase”
- “Clear”
- “Options”
- “X” icon at far right

Bottom-left video/control icons present, very small and unlabeled.

Main slide text:

**Dorsal lesions**
- **Optic ataxia**
  - Impairment of visually guided reaching
  - despite normal object recognition.
  - Impaired vision for action.

**Ventral lesions**  (blue text)
- Impairments in object recognition with spared
- vision for action.
- More counter-intuitive: Rare case studies

Title: “Seeing without a ventral strea” (final word cut off; likely truncated on screen)

Text:

- “Achromatopsia”
- “Unable to read, recognize people or objects,”
- “discern simple texture boundaries.”
- “Educated at school for visually impaired.”
